# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

const json = (body: unknown, status = 200, headers: HeadersInit = {}) =>
  new Response(JSON.stringify(body), {
    status,
    headers: {
      "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
      ...headers,
    },
  });

const text = (body: string, status = 200, headers: HeadersInit = {}) =>
  new Response(body, {
    status,
    headers: {
      "content-type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
      ...headers,
    },
  });

const ALLOWED_HOSTS = new Set([
  "example.com",
  "api.example.com",
]);

function isAllowedUrl(input: string): URL | null {
  let url: URL;
  try {
    url = new URL(input);
  } catch {
    return null;
  }

  if (!["http:", "https:"].includes(url.protocol)) return null;
  if (url.username || url.password) return null;
  if (!ALLOWED_HOSTS.has(url.hostname)) return null;

  return url;
}

Deno.serve(async (req) => {
  if (req.method !== "GET") {
    return text("Method Not Allowed", 405, { allow: "GET" });
  }

  const requestUrl = new URL(req.url);
  const target = requestUrl.searchParams.get("url");

  if (!target) {
    return json({ error: 'Missing required "url" query parameter' }, 400);
  }

  const safeUrl = isAllowedUrl(target);
  if (!safeUrl) {
    return json({ error: "URL is not allowed" }, 400);
  }

  const perm = await Deno.permissions.request({
    name: "net",
    host: safeUrl.hostname,
  });

  if (perm.state !== "granted") {
    return json({ error: "Network permission denied" }, 403);
  }

  try {
    const upstream = await fetch(safeUrl, {
      redirect: "follow",
      headers: {
        "user-agent": "DenoDeployFetcher/1.0",
        "accept": "*/*",
      },
    });

    const body = await upstream.text();

    return new Response(body, {
      status: upstream.status,
      headers: {
        "content-type": upstream.headers.get("content-type") ?? "text/plain; charset=utf-8",
        "x-fetched-url": safeUrl.toString(),
      },
    });
  } catch {
    return json({ error: "Failed to fetch upstream URL" }, 502);
  }
});
